text.title: Elasticsearch Index Lifecycle Management and Templates Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: First indexed log creates 100+ field mappings with default `text+keyword`; storage doubles; query speed halves.
antipattern.root-cause: Author indexed before defining a template.
antipattern.fix: Define index template up-front; reindex (or accept the bad mapping until rollover).

antipattern.symptom: Some days produce 1 GB index; other days 200 GB; shard sizes wildly uneven; warm-tier migration confusing.
antipattern.root-cause: Author used `logs-YYYY.MM.dd` naming without rollover thresholds.
antipattern.fix: Replace with rollover-driven naming (logs-000001, logs-000002…) + write alias.

antipattern.symptom: Forgotten cron job; disk fills; cluster red; emergency triage at 2 a.m.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wrote a one-off bash cron; never reviewed.
antipattern.fix: Replace cron with ILM policy. Verify via `GET _ilm/explain` that indices are progressing through phases.

antipattern.symptom: Search slow + heap pressure; cluster has 20k shards on 6 nodes.
antipattern.root-cause: Author left default 5 shards + per-day index.
antipattern.fix: Reduce to 1-shard default + rollover; force-merge older indices to 1 segment (warm phase action).
